Alright, welcome back. In this lesson I just want to go over four core concepts that you're going to hear quite throughout Min Min Bible Launch 2.0. So just to give you an initial overview, we're going to certainly be repeating them throughout. So this is just a brief introduction. But everything is essentially based on the idea of exclusive empowerment, of empowered consent, this idea of non anonymity, which is always a hard word to say, and automated intimacy. So let's go through all of them in a little bit more detail. So exclusive empowerment as it relates to the offer simply means someone having access to, you know, a better offer, right? That could be additional bonuses, that could be co-created bonuses based on conversations you've had with them, within the strategies you're using. So in the mid-card example, right, in the mid-card temperature check, if they email you saying what their concern is, you're able to co-create a version, right, and give them either an extended guarantee, you could give them a certain bonus in your vault that addresses some of that. So that would be a form of an exclusively empowered offer likewise in the early invitation in strategy one, offering them the surprise early bird bonus code, right, whether that's a coupon code or an early bird price that they get access to before the main card opens. That is a form of an exclusively empowered offer, right, and people want to take advantage of things they have exclusive access to. So this is the idea behind number one and how we're going to be using it throughout the various strategies. Next is empowered consent, right, we spoke all about how in the first strategy and in the mid-card temperature check and even in the last closing phase of your sequence, you are going to be looking for consent, making sure that they are really wanting and desiring to hear about this offer and to participate in this launch. So having that is going to establish trust and what I call for lack of better term. And I really do hate this term, but a mid-cro commitment. So it's not a micro commitment. It's somewhere in the middle, right, someone who has raised their hand, click to link, click to bun that said, yes, I'm interested in hearing more about this offer. Yes, I want to participate in this launch. Obviously, that's not the same as a yes, I want this product, but it's not a benign limp, wristed, yes, either, that's somewhere in the middle in terms of a commitment level. So getting consent, but in a way that is empowering, right, having them opt in and raise their hand, which is closely correlated to the yes of your product is a very powerful strategy that we are going to be implementing in strategies one and two. Next is the idea of non anonymity for coaching decisiveness. So one of the greatest reasons for not making a decision is simply not feeling accountable to one and you don't feel accountable to one. If you feel anonymous within the relationship. So the classic example is a massive launch, right, where you're getting these emails, you're clicking through on the sales page, but it doesn't feel like you're actually in relationship to that brand, to that company, to that influencer, to that coach, right? You're kind of feeling anonymous to them. And because you feel anonymous to them, you don't feel accountable for a decision. Whereas if you felt like you were actually in partnership and relationship being invited personally and actually seen and accounted for within that funnel, it adds a layer of appropriate accountability because all partnerships and your offer should be a partnership. Have a layer of accountability built within them. So we are going to work on making your potential customers, clients, students non anonymous. And in doing so, encouraging more decisions and more decisions always yields more conversions. So we're going to go deeper on to that in strategies four and five. And then finally, we have the idea of automated intimacy. And this is really about creating these conversion contexts, as I call them, where you know how to intervene at a touch point to really coach that conversion one step further, right? So you can't reach out to everybody on your list, that would just be probably that should crazy and terrible, even if you do have a small list, and it wouldn't even be welcomed to a certain degree if you're messaging someone who isn't actively engaged in that decision making process. However, you can use automation, things like lead scoring, for example, to really know and be able to tell who is active in that decision making process. Someone who would welcome and actually feel supported by a more intimate exchange with you or someone in someone with your team. So intimacy requires a degree of automation to be able to do at scale. And in strategies four and five, we are going to show you some of our favorite ways to do that within a launch context. So that is what we have in store. These are the strategies that these are the core concepts that are layered into the five strategies that we are going to be working through in the remainder of the program. Those once again, being the exclusively empowered early access trigger, which we are going to learn next, then the mid cart temperature check, the slice of life closing template, the minority report reach out, which leans on that concept of automated intimacy and some lead scoring. And then finally, the personalized proposal. So that's what we have in store. Take a breather. And when you're ready, meet us in the next lesson where we will dive into that exclusively empowered early access trigger. I'll see you there.
